title: Verifying a proof (the moat)
purpose: Show that anyone, on any machine, re-checks a proof with no Troof server involved — and that one changed byte flips it red.

style.verdict: green=Verified / red=Tampered are the visual climax

steps.label: Re-fetch
steps.detail: GET from a PUBLIC Walrus aggregator (no Troof server)

steps.label: Re-hash
steps.detail: SHA-256 of the fetched canonical bundle, computed in-browser

steps.label: Read anchor
steps.detail: fetch the on-chain Record's content_hash from Sui

outcomes.label: TAMPERED
outcomes.trigger: change one byte of the report

annotations: Show the three hashes stacked (re-hashed / anchored on Sui / sealed in blob) all equal in green; in the tampered variant the first differs.
Caption: 'No competitor's AI answer can be re-checked. That's the point.'
